title: Matrix2x2 with Arithmetic, I/O, and Comparison
prompt_md:
## Goal

Build a `Matrix2x2` class that integrates with the language like a built-in type, combining several techniques from this chapter: friend arithmetic, member comparison, and friend stream I/O.

## Input

- Line 1: four integers `a b c d` -- the first matrix in row-major order (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right).
- Line 2: four integers `e f g h` -- the second matrix.
- Line 3: one of `add`, `subtract`, or `compare`.

## Output

- For `add` / `subtract`, print the resulting matrix with `operator<<` in this exact format (each row on its own line, ending with a newline):
  ```
  [a, b]
  [c, d]
  ```
- For `compare`, print `Equal` if the two matrices are element-wise equal, otherwise `Not equal`.

Provide: `operator+` and `operator-` as friend functions; `operator==` as a member; `operator<<` and `operator>>` as friends (the input operator reads four integers into a matrix). The provided `main` reads both matrices with `>>` and dispatches on the command. Use only `<iostream>` and `<string>`.

title: Accumulator Functor with Subscript and Typecast
prompt_md:
## Goal

Build an `Accumulator` class that combines three operator-overloading techniques on one type: it is a functor (`operator()` appends a value), it supports indexed reads (`operator[]`), and it converts to `int` (explicit typecast) yielding the sum of all stored values.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer N (1 <= N <= 20), the number of commands.
- Next N lines, each one of:
  - `push X` -- append integer X by calling the accumulator as a functor.
  - `get I` -- read the element at 0-based index I via `operator[]`.
  - `sum` -- the total of all stored values via the typecast to `int`.

## Output

- For each `get I`: print `acc[I] = V`.
- For each `sum`: print `Sum = S`.
- `push` produces no output.

Store the values in a `std::vector<int>`. `operator()(int)` pushes (returns void). `operator[](int) const` returns the element (assert the index is valid). `explicit operator int() const` returns the sum (you may use `std::accumulate`). The provided `main` parses commands and uses `static_cast<int>(acc)` for sums. You may include `<iostream>`, `<string>`, `<vector>`, `<numeric>`, `<cassert>`.

test_cases.label: Sample -- push, get, and sum
test_cases.stdin:
7
push 10
push 20
push 30
get 0
get 2
sum
push 40

test_cases.label: Hidden -- sum after many pushes including negatives
test_cases.stdin:
6
push 5
push -3
push 8
push -10
sum
get 1

test_cases.label: Hidden -- running sums interleaved
test_cases.stdin:
6
push 1
sum
push 2
sum
push 3
sum
